subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0. State-aligned outlets (Straits Times/SPH, CNA) were EXCLUDED on governance/rights/press and rebuilt from independent monitors (Freedom House, RSF, WJP, HRW, Transparency International); economics anchored to the IMF Article IV.
The core tension: world-class capacity, safety, and finance against a dominant-party state with low press freedom, restricted civil liberties, and a hard path to belonging.

Source-lean: economics IMF-anchored; rule_of_law kept net-positive only because the corruption/commercial/regulatory strength is corroborated by independent WJP/TI data, with rights sub-dimensions scored down.

economic.notes: A world-class financial/trade hub with deep reserves and sovereign funds; tail risk only from external-demand exposure.

fiscal_state.notes: Surpluses, large reserves plus GIC/Temasek sovereign funds, AAA-rated. (IMF Art IV 2025)

monetary_independence.notes: The MAS runs an exchange-rate-centred regime (managed float vs a basket) - deliberate, highly credible, and effective at anchoring inflation. (IMF Art IV 2025; MAS)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The SGD is not a major reserve currency but is a hard, stable currency in a top global financial hub. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: World-class hub, CAR ~19%, strong liquidity/asset quality; the IMF judges it resilient. (IMF Art IV 2025)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: An entrepot/finance/electronics hub with ultra-open trade and no resource curse; tail risk only from external-demand dependence. (IMF)

institutional.notes: World-class state capacity, commercial law, and anti-corruption, against constrained political/judicial space and low press freedom.

rule_of_law.notes: A split: excellent on corruption, regulatory enforcement, and commercial/contract law (WJP 16/143; 3rd globally on absence of corruption), but weak on constraints on government and fundamental rights. Net high. (WJP; TI CPI 84/100)

statistical_integrity.notes: Credible, IMF-validated economic data. (IMF Art IV 2025)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A world-class meritocratic bureaucracy and execution - a genuine strength. (IMF; WJP regulatory enforcement)

press_freedom.notes: RSF 123/180 and falling: state-aligned media, POFMA, and defamation suits. Clearly negative. (RSF 2025; Freedom House)

political_social.notes: Very high regime stability and order, against restricted civil liberties and a hard path to belonging.

political_stability.notes: Very high: the PAP won ~66% in May 2025 with an orderly 2024 transition to PM Wong; discounted long-run for one-party dominance with no pluralist release valve. (Freedom House 2025; election results)

civil_liberties.notes: 'Partly Free': restricted assembly/speech/association, judicial caning, and executions for drug offences. (Freedom House 2025; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Selective lawful entry (the high-threshold Employment Pass, COMPASS points), orderly, but PR/citizenship is hard with a limited path to belonging - a ceiling. (MOM/EDB 2025)

social_cohesion.notes: Orderly multi-ethnic management and very low crime. (observed)

demographics.notes: Fertility ~0.97 (below 1.0), with 65+ rising toward ~24% by 2030; structurally immigration-dependent. (SingStat)

geopolitical.notes: A US security partner and ASEAN hub that deliberately balances China; low direct conflict exposure.

alliance_reliability.notes: A US security partner and ASEAN hub that deliberately balances China - reliable but non-aligned by design. (CFR)

conflict_involvement.notes: Low direct involvement; South China Sea adjacency and great-power competition are background risks to a trade-dependent hub. (CFR)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An open capital account and free hub - positive for holders. (IMF; MAS)

physical_practical.notes: World-class infrastructure, healthcare, and safety; the rising tail is sea-level for a low island city-state.

climate.notes: ~30% of the island is under 5m elevation with significant sea-level and heat exposure; strong adaptation (S$100bn+ committed) but rising long-run risk. (NCCS; PUB)

healthcare.notes: A world-class system, medical-tourism grade. (IMF; observed)

infrastructure.notes: World-class port, airport, telecom, and utilities. (IMF Art IV)

crime_safety.notes: Among the safest globally - a genuine strength. (observed)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low natural-catastrophe exposure (no quakes/cyclones) with a developed market and high state capacity; sea-level the rising tail. (observed)

summary: A world-class financial/trade hub with deep fiscal buffers, a uniquely credible monetary regime, the strongest state capacity and anti-corruption record, world-class infrastructure and healthcare, and among the lowest crime anywhere. The offsets are a dominant-party state with low press freedom, restricted civil liberties (caning, drug-offence executions), a hard path to citizenship/belonging for its large foreign workforce, very low fertility, and rising sea-level exposure for a low island.
